exercises.prompt: In a single row, report the total number of cards as total_cards, how many have an ATK value as with_atk, and how many are missing one as missing_atk.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_cards, COUNT(atk) AS with_atk, COUNT(*) - COUNT(atk) AS missing_atk FROM cards
hints: COUNT(*) counts every row, but COUNT(atk) skips the NULLs — the gap between them is the missing count.
Select COUNT(*), COUNT(atk), and COUNT(*) - COUNT(atk).

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_cards, COUNT(atk) AS with_atk, COUNT(*) - COUNT(atk) AS missing_atk FROM cards
```

exercises.prompt: Some cards belong to no archetype at all. Replace a missing archetype with the text '(none)' and count how many cards fall under each archetype label. Return the label as archetype_label and the count as card_count for the 5 largest groups, most numerous first, breaking ties alphabetically by archetype_label.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT COALESCE(archetype, '(none)') AS archetype_label, COUNT(*) AS card_count FROM cards GROUP BY archetype_label ORDER BY card_count DESC, archetype_label LIMIT 5
hints: COALESCE(archetype, '(none)') returns the archetype when present and '(none)' when it is NULL; you can group by that expression.
GROUP BY COALESCE(archetype, '(none)'), ORDER BY card_count DESC, archetype_label, LIMIT 5.

```sql
SELECT COALESCE(archetype, '(none)') AS archetype_label, COUNT(*) AS card_count FROM cards GROUP BY archetype_label ORDER BY card_count DESC, archetype_label LIMIT 5
```

exercises.prompt: The Egyptian Gods have variable ATK that the data stores as NULL. List every DIVINE-attribute card that has no ATK value — return just the name, ordered by name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT name FROM cards WHERE attribute = 'DIVINE' AND atk IS NULL ORDER BY name
hints: A missing value is tested with IS NULL, never with = NULL.
WHERE attribute = 'DIVINE' AND atk IS NULL, then ORDER BY name.

```sql
SELECT name FROM cards WHERE attribute = 'DIVINE' AND atk IS NULL ORDER BY name
```

exercises.prompt: For Link Monsters, compare two averages of atk in one row: avg_real_atk, the average over only the cards that have an ATK value, and avg_with_zeros, the average when every missing ATK is treated as 0. Round both to 1 decimal place.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(atk), 1) AS avg_real_atk, ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(atk, 0)), 1) AS avg_with_zeros FROM cards WHERE type = 'Link Monster'
hints: AVG(atk) ignores NULLs, but AVG(COALESCE(atk, 0)) first turns each missing value into 0 so it drags the average down.
Select ROUND(AVG(atk), 1) and ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(atk, 0)), 1), filtered WHERE type = 'Link Monster'.

```sql
SELECT ROUND(AVG(atk), 1) AS avg_real_atk, ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(atk, 0)), 1) AS avg_with_zeros FROM cards WHERE type = 'Link Monster'
```

exercises.prompt: Split every card into two groups by whether it has an ATK value: label it 'Has ATK' when atk is present and 'No ATK' when it is NULL. Return the label as atk_status and the count as card_count, most numerous first, breaking ties alphabetically by atk_status.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT CASE WHEN atk IS NULL THEN 'No ATK' ELSE 'Has ATK' END AS atk_status, COUNT(*) AS card_count FROM cards GROUP BY atk_status ORDER BY card_count DESC, atk_status
hints: A CASE whose test is 'atk IS NULL' turns the presence or absence of a value into a label you can group by.
CASE WHEN atk IS NULL THEN 'No ATK' ELSE 'Has ATK' END AS atk_status, then GROUP BY atk_status.

```sql
SELECT CASE WHEN atk IS NULL THEN 'No ATK' ELSE 'Has ATK' END AS atk_status, COUNT(*) AS card_count FROM cards GROUP BY atk_status ORDER BY card_count DESC, atk_status
```

exercises.prompt: Which monster card types have members with no level? Considering only cards whose type contains the word Monster, report for each type the total number of cards as total_cards and how many are missing a level as missing_level, keeping only types where at least one card is missing its level. Order by missing_level descending, breaking ties alphabetically by type.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT type, COUNT(*) AS total_cards, COUNT(*) - COUNT(level) AS missing_level FROM cards WHERE type LIKE '%Monster%' GROUP BY type HAVING COUNT(*) - COUNT(level) > 0 ORDER BY missing_level DESC, type
hints: COUNT(*) - COUNT(level) is the number of NULL levels in each group, and you can filter groups on it with HAVING.
GROUP BY type HAVING COUNT(*) - COUNT(level) > 0, then ORDER BY missing_level DESC, type.

```sql
SELECT type, COUNT(*) AS total_cards, COUNT(*) - COUNT(level) AS missing_level FROM cards WHERE type LIKE '%Monster%' GROUP BY type HAVING COUNT(*) - COUNT(level) > 0 ORDER BY missing_level DESC, type
```
